title: The One With Monicas Thunder

jokes.text: She's been there for 20 minutes. You didn't hear? I thought it was a kid yelling, 'I'm gay!'
jokes.setup: Monica has been loudly announcing her engagement for 20 minutes
jokes.punchline: Chandler mishearing 'I'm engaged' as 'I'm gay'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler wit with perfect misdirection. Strong setup/payoff structure and very quotable. The mishearing feels both unexpected and believable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm engaged!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Wait, you're gay?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, I'm ENGAGED!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Right, right, I heard you the first time.

jokes.text: No, that guy moved.
jokes.setup: Chandler's joke about thinking Monica sounds like a gay kid
jokes.punchline: Joey matter-of-factly confirming there actually was a gay kid who moved
jokes.explanation: Good topper that extends the previous joke. Joey's oblivious literalness works well here.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: There was.

jokes.text: Oh, big talk! Why don't you come here and say that to me?
jokes.setup: Monica is yelling about being engaged from the balcony
jokes.punchline: Monica picking a fight with a neighbor over her excitement
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's competitive nature even in celebration. Solid character moment but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm engaged! I'm engaged!

dialogue.speaker: Neighbor
dialogue.line: Some of us are trying to sleep!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, some of us are getting married!

jokes.text: My fiancé will kick your ass.
jokes.setup: Monica threatening to fight a neighbor
jokes.punchline: Monica invoking Chandler as her protector
jokes.explanation: Continues the escalation but feels predictable. The comedy comes from the absurdity of the situation.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You want to mess with me? I've got Chandler!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Why would you say that?

jokes.text: You get her in, you bolt the door, I'll be in the closet.
jokes.setup: Monica is threatening neighbors and causing a scene
jokes.punchline: Chandler's cowardly escape plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - self-aware cowardice delivered with his characteristic timing.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Could I BE any more terrified of Monica right now?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm just gonna slowly back away and pretend I don't know her.

jokes.text: It just wouldn't be my night... Our night... if you weren't here to celebrate with me... Us. Damn it!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to be gracious and include Chandler in her speech
jokes.punchline: Monica repeatedly forgetting to include Chandler and getting frustrated
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Monica's self-centered nature even when trying to be inclusive. The escalating frustration works well.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I want to thank everyone who helped make this day special. Rachel, for being my best friend. Phoebe, for your wonderful songs. Joey, for... well, for being Joey.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What about me?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And Chandler, of course! And Ross, my brother, who walked me down the aisle.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm already forgotten?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No! I also want to thank the amazing caterer, the photographer, the florist...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What?! Oh my God, and Chandler! How could I forget Chandler, my wonderful husband!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And I especially want to thank my parents for their support, and my bridesmaids who looked absolutely beautiful...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Are you kidding me right now?

jokes.text: Speaking of chiming in, remember the time you burned down my apartment?
jokes.setup: Phoebe offers to play music at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Monica bringing up Phoebe's past apartment fire as a reason to decline
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Monica's grudge-holding nature. Works for long-time viewers.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I could play music at your wedding!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's so sweet, but... remember when your apartment caught on fire?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: That was ONE time!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Exactly. I'd like my wedding to be ONE time too.

jokes.text: So when you said get up early, did you mean 1986?
jokes.setup: Joey says he needs to look good for an audition to play a 19-year-old
jokes.punchline: Chandler suggesting Joey would need to go back in time to look 19
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure with perfect timing. The math works (Joey would have been 19 around 1986) and it's very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I need to look good for this audition. They're looking for someone who can play 19.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, you're gonna need a time machine.

jokes.text: Oh, 19! We thought you said 90.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if they think he looks 19
jokes.punchline: Pretending they misheard 19 as 90
jokes.explanation: Solid follow-up joke but feels like a standard sitcom topper. Less creative than Chandler's 1986 line.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Do you think I look 19?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: 90? Yeah, sure, buddy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Nineteen? No way. You look way older than that.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, definitely not 19.

jokes.text: You know what shoes would look great with this ring? Diamond shoes.
jokes.setup: Monica admiring her engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Monica wanting diamond shoes to match
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's materialistic excitement perfectly. The absurdity of diamond shoes works for her character.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Look at this ring! It's absolutely gorgeous.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You know what would be perfect? Diamond shoes to match!

jokes.text: Fashion is about me? About youth. Youth! Listen up, grandpa.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining that fashion is about youth
jokes.punchline: Joey mishearing 'youth' as 'you' and Rachel calling him grandpa
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that shows Joey's confusion. The 'grandpa' callback to his age works adequately.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Fashion is about youth.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Fashion is about me?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No, youth!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh, okay.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Come on, Grandpa.

jokes.text: I'm not worried. I'm fascinated. You know? It's like biology. Which is funny, because in high school I failed biology... and tonight biology failed me.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to comfort Chandler about sexual dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Chandler's parallel between failing biology and biology failing him
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly crafted joke with perfect parallel structure. Classic Chandler - using wordplay to cope with embarrassment. Highly quotable and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: It's okay, it happens to a lot of guys.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, it's never happened to me before. I failed biology in high school, but biology never failed me.

jokes.text: Twelve years old and addicted to pork, No one thought Monica would marry
jokes.setup: Phoebe offering to sing at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate wedding song lyrics about Monica's childhood
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - completely inappropriate but delivered with sincere enthusiasm. The dark humor about childhood Monica is very original.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I'd love to sing at your wedding! I have the perfect song. It's called 'Monica Geller Had a Messy Apartment' to the tune of 'The Star-Spangled Banner.'

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe, no.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh come on, it's beautiful! 'Oh say can you see, all the stuff piled up to the ceiling, with your mother's obsession and your need for perfection, your apartment's a time bomb that's—'

jokes.text: First time I met Chandler I thought he was gay, But here I am singing On his wedding day
jokes.setup: Phoebe's second attempt at a wedding song
jokes.punchline: Singing about initially thinking Chandler was gay
jokes.explanation: Another inappropriate Phoebe song, though less original than the first. Good callback to the ongoing Chandler gay jokes.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, I thought you were gay. But then I realized you were just metrosexual. You're very well groomed and you have a skincare routine. But then I realized you were just British!

jokes.text: If you'd let me finish, it goes on to say that he's probably not gay.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler's horrified reaction to the gay song
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual clarification that the song eventually says he's probably not gay
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe moment - the casual 'probably' makes it even worse. Her complete lack of awareness is perfectly executed.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, well, the song does say at the end that he's probably not gay.

jokes.text: You guys don't have this problem, you're made of wood.
jokes.setup: Chandler's sexual dysfunction conversation in front of wooden furniture
jokes.punchline: Chandler envying furniture for not having erectile dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Absurd but clever observation that fits Chandler's coping mechanism. The visual element adds to the humor.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know what the worst part about having erectile dysfunction is? It's not the obvious thing. It's that you're standing there with a beautiful woman, and all you can think about is wooden furniture. Because at least the furniture gets to be hard.

jokes.text: Well, sure, but I get married all the time.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Ross can believe Monica and Chandler are getting married
jokes.punchline: Ross casually referencing his multiple failed marriages
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware joke about Ross's relationship history. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, let's see how long that lasts.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I give it... three years? Maybe four if they really work at it.

jokes.text: You mean 'we,' you and me? Oh, no, no, no. 'We,' you with someone, me with someone. Good, you scared me a minute.
jokes.setup: Rachel asking if they'll ever have what Monica and Chandler have
jokes.punchline: The mutual horror at the thought of being together
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke that plays on their complicated history. The mutual relief is well-executed.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Do you think we'll ever have what Monica and Chandler have?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh God, no.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Yeah, me neither.

jokes.text: It's just because you and I, we were like a nightmare.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel's relief about not being together
jokes.punchline: Ross describing their relationship as a nightmare
jokes.explanation: Straightforward self-deprecating humor but lacks creativity. Gets the point across but not memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm actually relieved we're not together anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Really? Me too.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yeah, I mean, our relationship was a nightmare. Do you know how many times I had to explain paleontology to you?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, please. You were the nightmare. You were always so... so Ross.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: So Ross?! That's not even an insult!

jokes.text: Surely you can think of something. Just give me a minute.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying there were some good times in their relationship
jokes.punchline: Ross struggling to think of any good memories
jokes.explanation: Simple awkward moment that plays on their relationship troubles. The timing beats work adequately.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: There were some really good times in our relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: ...Yeah?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Like... like when?

jokes.text: You were always good at the... stuff.
jokes.setup: Ross finally thinking of something good about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly referring to sex as 'stuff'
jokes.explanation: Awkward euphemism that fits Ross's character. The discomfort is palpable but not exceptionally crafted.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, the stuff... I mean, we had really great stuff.

jokes.text: I was good at the stuff? I really liked your hands.
jokes.setup: Ross's awkward compliment about 'stuff'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's equally awkward response about liking his hands
jokes.explanation: Continues the awkward sexual reminiscing. The hand comment is oddly specific but not particularly funny.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I like your stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I like your hands.

jokes.text: 'Sup? 'Sup, dude? Take whatever you want, just don't hurt me.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to look 19 with '90s slang and style
jokes.punchline: Chandler thinking Joey is a criminal trying to rob him
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy moment. Joey's attempt to look young makes him look threatening instead. Good misdirection.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yo, yo, yo! What's up, my man? You got any bling bling? I'm about to go to the club and get jiggy with it!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, first of all, stop talking. Second of all, why are you dressed like you're about to commit a crime? And third of all, if you're here to rob me, I should tell you I have nothing of value except this half-eaten sandwich.

jokes.text: That's whack. PlayStation is whack. 'Sup with the whack PlayStation, 'sup?
jokes.setup: Joey trying to talk like a teenager
jokes.punchline: Overusing '90s slang awkwardly and incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Shows Joey's desperation to seem young through terrible slang usage. The repetition gets slightly tedious.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yo, that is totally da bomb, fo' shizzle!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: All that and a bag of chips, am I right?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: That's whack! Totally tight!

jokes.text: Yes, on a scale of one to ten, ten being the dumbest a person can look... you are definitely 19.
jokes.setup: Joey asking if he looks 19
jokes.punchline: Chandler's backhanded compliment about looking stupid
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Chandler sarcasm. The structure builds to a great insult disguised as agreement.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Do I look 19?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah... you look like you're 19 and stupid.

jokes.text: Young. You're a man-child, okay? Now get changed, everybody's ready. And please, please, keep my underwear.
jokes.setup: Joey asking how old he looks in Chandler's clothes
jokes.punchline: Chandler letting Joey keep his underwear rather than have it back
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that works for the situation. The desperation in Chandler's voice sells the joke.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: How old do I look in these clothes?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, the good news is you look like you're in your thirties. The bad news is you're wearing my underwear.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: So? You can keep them.

jokes.text: When, today? Some, not a lot.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if Joey has had a lot of sex
jokes.punchline: Joey thinking Chandler means today specifically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - completely literal interpretation. Great economy and timing on the response.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Have you had a lot of sex?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Today? No, not today.

jokes.text: I cannot believe I'm asking advice from somebody who's wearing that hat.
jokes.setup: Chandler seeking advice about sexual dysfunction from Joey
jokes.punchline: Chandler's realization about how low he's sunk based on Joey's ridiculous outfit
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment that acknowledges the visual absurdity. Chandler's desperation comes through.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, I need your advice about something personal.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Sure, buddy. What's up?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Well, I'm having some... performance issues. In the bedroom.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh man, that's rough. But hey, you came to the right guy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Did I? Because you're wearing a backwards tuxedo t-shirt, and that's when I realized I've hit rock bottom.

jokes.text: Well, what'd you do? I did it anyway.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking what Joey did when he had erectile dysfunction
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact response about proceeding anyway
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Joey's casual attitude about sexual problems fits his character perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What did you do when you had erectile dysfunction?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I went ahead anyway.

jokes.text: But if you touch my guitar again, I'll have to pound on you a little bit.
jokes.setup: Phoebe agreeing to let Monica play guitar at the wedding
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual threat of violence over her guitar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe - mixing sweetness with unexpected aggression. The casual 'little bit' makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: You can play guitar at my wedding, but if you mess up, I will kill you. And then I will dig you up and kill you again.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, apparently I opened the door to the past.
jokes.setup: Monica walking in on Ross and Rachel kissing
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic reaction to their romantic history
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor that acknowledges the show's history. Monica's dramatic delivery works well.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Oh my God! Oh my God!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Finally! I've been waiting for this for like ten years!

jokes.text: See, you kind of stole my thunder.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining why she's upset about Ross and Rachel kissing
jokes.punchline: The phrase 'stealing thunder' which becomes the episode's central theme
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main theme and title. Monica's competitiveness shows perfectly through this phrase.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You guys kissed?! That's huge! I was gonna tell everyone at dinner tonight that I'm getting married, and now nobody's gonna care because you two are gonna be all 'We kissed, we kissed!' And nobody's gonna want to hear about my wedding!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Monica, come on, that's not—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: You're stealing my thunder!

jokes.text: Thunder being stolen.
jokes.setup: Phoebe getting excited about Ross and Rachel's kiss
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan commentary on her thunder being stolen
jokes.explanation: Good running gag establishment. Monica's flat delivery of her own victimization is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, oh my God! Did you see that? Ross and Rachel just kissed!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, I was there. And I'm thrilled for them, I really am. But could they have picked literally any other moment? I just got engaged!

jokes.text: I cannot believe you guys are talking about this. Problems in the bedroom are between a man and a woman. All right? Now, Chandler is doing the best he can.
jokes.setup: Everyone discussing Ross and Rachel's kiss
jokes.punchline: Joey thinking they're discussing Chandler's sexual problems
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Joey's confusion. His protective stance about Chandler's 'best' is endearing and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You guys are talking about Chandler's sexual problems?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: No, Joey, we're talking about Ross and Rachel's kiss.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Oh. So... no sexual problems?

jokes.text: What a great night. Chandler can't do it, they kissed.
jokes.setup: Monica's frustration building about her ruined engagement night
jokes.punchline: Monica sarcastically listing everything that went wrong
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's mounting frustration effectively. The blunt summary works for her direct personality.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, that's great! That's just great! My engagement night is ruined, Chandler's been kidnapped by a runaway bride, and now I'm standing here in this dress that cost more than my first apartment, and nobody even noticed!

jokes.text: Seriously? Seriously, no. Okay? You can play your own age, which is 31. I'm 30! Joey, you are not. You're 31. Oh, crap!
jokes.setup: Joey asking multiple times if he can pass for 19
jokes.punchline: Rachel finally being brutally honest, then Joey realizing he forgot his own age
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Joey's confusion about his own age being the final punchline. Very Joey-like moment.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Come on, Rachel. Do you really think I could pass for 19?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I mean, if I really wanted to, couldn't I pull it off?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Rachel, seriously, be honest with me. Could I pass for 19?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Joey, you're 30 years old.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know that! I'm asking if I could pass for 19!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: No. You look like a man in his thirties who's been through some stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Wait... how old am I again?

jokes.text: That is whack.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she doesn't want to talk especially to Rachel
jokes.punchline: Joey still using the terrible '90s slang inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Simple callback to Joey's slang attempts. Gets a mild laugh but nothing special.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! That is NOT cool. You gotta talk to Rachel. She's your friend. And more importantly, she's my... she's my friend too. So here's what we're gonna do - we're gonna sit down, we're gonna have some coffee, and we're gonna work this out. Word to your mother!

jokes.text: It's starting to sound like 'wood cheese.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe repeating 'Would she?' multiple times
jokes.punchline: The phrase morphing into 'wood cheese'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's very Phoebe-like. The absurdity of 'wood cheese' is genuinely funny.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Would she? Would she? Would she? Would she? Wood she? Wood cheese?

jokes.text: Man, I could go for some wood cheese right now.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's 'wood cheese' observation
jokes.punchline: Joey actually wanting to eat the nonsensical food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - treating imaginary food as real. His food obsession makes this believable and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wood cheese? That's not a real thing.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I don't care, I'd eat it. I'd eat anything right now.

jokes.text: We will sprinkle rose petals on the bed and make love... not just because it's romantic, but because I can.
jokes.setup: Chandler fantasizing about his honeymoon
jokes.punchline: Referencing his current sexual dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Good callback that turns romantic fantasy into dysfunction joke. The hope in his voice makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm picturing us on a beach in Fiji, the sun setting, soft music playing... Monica in that little black dress... and me just lying there thinking, 'Well, at least the scenery is nice.'

jokes.text: They give you away. There's too much wisdom in there.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining what's wrong with Joey's eyes for looking young
jokes.punchline: Joey having too much wisdom in his eyes
jokes.explanation: Ironic given Joey's usual lack of intelligence. The absurdity of Joey having 'too much wisdom' is amusing.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Your eyes are too wise.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Too wise?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Yeah, you have too much wisdom in your eyes. You look old.

jokes.text: My sweet 16. You went to third base with my cousin Charlie.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining previous thunder-stealing incidents
jokes.punchline: Specific embarrassing memory from her teenage years
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's long memory for grievances. The specific 'third base' detail adds humor.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm not a thunder stealer! I just... I have a lot of embarrassing memories from my teenage years that tend to come up at inopportune moments.

jokes.text: The only reason I did that was because your party was so boring. We had a caricaturist.
jokes.setup: Ross defending his behavior at Monica's sweet 16
jokes.punchline: Monica's proud defense of having a caricaturist
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between boring party entertainment and Monica's pride in it. Shows her earnestness perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I was just trying to help! The caricaturist was making everyone look ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That's what caricaturists do, Ross! And he was amazing. He made me look like a beautiful woman with a huge forehead.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: That's not amazing, Monica, that's accurate.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, I thought it was great! I'm keeping that drawing forever.

jokes.text: Easy, mimey, the moment has passed. It ain't gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel about to have sex
jokes.punchline: Chandler calling Ross a mime and declaring the romantic moment over
jokes.explanation: Great visual reference and timing. Chandler's casual dismissal of their romantic moment is perfectly timed.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey! Hey! You're a mime! You're a mime!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The romantic moment is over.

jokes.text: Maybe you can't stand... that your formerly fat friend is getting married before you.
jokes.setup: Monica theorizing why Rachel would steal her thunder
jokes.punchline: Monica bringing up her own past weight issues as ammunition
jokes.explanation: Shows Monica's competitiveness and self-awareness. Using her own insecurity as a weapon is very Monica.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: She's just jealous because I'm getting married first and she can't stand that I'm going to be the center of attention for once in my life.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: And if she tries to show me up, I will bring up the time she gained fifteen pounds and couldn't fit into her pants.

jokes.text: Come on, Ross. Let's go have sex!
jokes.setup: Rachel getting angry about Monica's accusations
jokes.punchline: Rachel loudly announcing fake sex plans for revenge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the conflict. Rachel's loudness and fake revenge sex plan works for the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, so now I'm the problem? Fine! You know what? I'm going to have sex on this couch!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to have sex in this chair!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to have sex in the kitchen on the counter!

jokes.text: Well, somebody should.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying she can't believe they'll have sex on Monica's engagement night
jokes.punchline: Monica referencing that she and Chandler can't because of his dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Efficient callback that turns the situation into self-pity. Good economy of words for maximum impact.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I can't believe you guys are going to have sex on your engagement night.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Well, we're not actually.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Chandler has erectile dysfunction.

jokes.text: Yeah, you like that, baby?
jokes.setup: Ross and Rachel pretending to have sex to upset Monica
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward fake sex sounds for Monica to overhear
jokes.explanation: Standard fake sex comedy. Gets the job done but lacks creativity or surprising elements.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, oh yeah, that's right!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Oh, oh, oh!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, yes, YES!

jokes.text: I warn you, the night you announce your engagement... I'm going to announce that I'm pregnant.
jokes.setup: Monica accepting Ross and Rachel getting back together
jokes.punchline: Monica's threat of future thunder-stealing revenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica revenge fantasy. Shows her competitive nature and planning ahead for petty revenge.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Oh, so you two are back together? That's great! I'm so happy for you! You know what? I'm going to remember this moment. And in about five years, when I do something really great, I'm going to expect you both to be just as excited for me. And if you're not, I'm going to be very, very upset. Actually, you know what? I'm going to steal your thunder anyway.

jokes.text: How is that ever going to happen?
jokes.setup: Monica's threat to announce pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Rachel pointing out Monica's not in a position to get pregnant
jokes.explanation: Logical response that references the ongoing dysfunction storyline. Simple but effective.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Monica, you're not exactly in a position to get pregnant.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: What? Why not?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Because you'd have to actually have sex with Chandler first.

jokes.text: I know that that's dumb, but you were so depressed... when Ross got married, you slept with Chandler.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she kissed Ross
jokes.punchline: Referencing Monica's past rebound behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback to major series event. Shows Rachel using Monica's own history against her effectively.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I kissed him because I needed to know if there was still something there, okay? And Monica does this thing where she just rebounds with whoever's around, and I didn't want to be that person.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I do not rebound!

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: You rebounded with like five guys after Richard.

jokes.text: I don't care, she slept with me.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Monica's past rebound behavior
jokes.punchline: Chandler's proud, simple response about being chosen
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment of simple pride. The delivery and timing make this much funnier than it should be.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She chose me.

jokes.text: From now on, the only person who's going to enjoy these bad boys is me.
jokes.setup: Chandler declaring Rachel missed her chance with him
jokes.punchline: Chandler referring to his own hands as 'bad boys'
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the hands discussion with typical Chandler self-referential humor. The 'bad boys' phrase is funny.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You missed your chance with me, Rachel. We had our moment.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: It's not going to happen again. I'm with Monica now, and these bad boys are off the market.

jokes.text: Hey, here's a dollar. Consider it a deposit. Please sing at our wedding.
jokes.setup: Phoebe singing another inappropriate wedding song
jokes.punchline: Chandler bribing her to stop with fake wedding promises
jokes.explanation: Decent resolution to the Phoebe wedding song subplot. Chandler's desperation comes through clearly.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay, Phoebe, what if I told you that Monica and I want you to sing at our vow renewal ceremony?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No. No, I'm bribing you to stop singing right now.

jokes.text: I'll pound on him in the morning.
jokes.setup: Phoebe continuing to sing after getting the dollar
jokes.punchline: Monica's delayed threat of violence against Chandler
jokes.explanation: Mild callback to Phoebe's phrasing. Shows Monica's frustration but not particularly strong as a closing joke.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: ♪ Oh, a tiny little man, and he wore a tiny little hat ♪

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay, Phoebe, that's enough.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: ♪ And he had a tiny little... ♪

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Phoebe!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: What? I'm just trying to help!

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: When I get my hands on you, Chandler Bing, I am gonna kill you.

tmdb_overview: The gang celebrate Monica and Chandler's engagement. Phoebe wants to be the musician at the wedding and comes up with a new song, while Joey tries to dress and act like a 19-year-old for an audition. Ross and Rachel briefly reunite and after Monica catches them kissing, she's convinced that Rachel is trying to steal her thunder.
insight: Season opener resets cast dynamics but lands below-average 71 score with thin standout moments.
